stories.body: Britain’s Ministry of Defence is investigating reports that the Russian frigate Admiral Grigorovich fired warning shots about 500 yards from a drifting UK-registered, motorless yacht between the Isle of Wight and Normandy after audible warnings failed, an encounter that occurred roughly 20 nautical miles south of the island amid fog and outside UK territorial waters while the warship was already being shadowed by HMS Mersey during routine Royal Navy monitoring, according to BBC News.
stories.headline: UK probes warning-shot incident between Russian frigate and British yacht in the Channel

stories.body: US Central Command said American forces shot down four Iranian drones believed to target maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz and then hit Iran’s surveillance sites at Goruk and Qeshm Island, escalating tensions as Washington and Tehran negotiate a broader ceasefire that would reopen the strait while Iran demands sanctions relief, port access and leverage over shipping amid a conflict that has spread to Lebanon through Hezbollah’s battles with Israel, Reuters reported.
stories.headline: US strikes Iranian coastal radar after intercepting drones over Strait of Hormuz

stories.body: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi told Lebanon’s Al Mayadeen that any interim agreement with the United States to end the wider Iran war must include a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah and an Israeli pullout from southern Lebanon, while Tehran reaffirmed support for the militia amid continued cross-border strikes, stalled US-brokered talks, and broader negotiations over sanctions relief and Strait of Hormuz control, according to Reuters.
stories.headline: Iran insists Hezbollah ceasefire and Israeli withdrawal are prerequisites for regional peace deal

stories.body: Kyiv Mayor Vitali Klitschko said Russian strikes early Tuesday ignited a nine-story apartment block in the Podil district, torched cars in Obolon and caused fires near a kindergarten, prompting renewed shelter warnings from authorities as President Volodymyr Zelenskiy cautioned citizens about the possibility of another massive attack following Moscow’s vow to hit decision-making centers in the capital, Reuters reported.
stories.headline: Kyiv endures heavy Russian missile barrage that sparks multiple fires

stories.body: Former Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko said his Pastef party, which holds a large parliamentary majority, will stay out of President Bassirou Diomaye Faye’s next government after disagreements over the party’s executive role, intensifying political rifts while Senegal tries to restart a frozen $1.8 billion IMF program in the wake of misreported debt that pushed liabilities to 132% of GDP, Reuters reported.
stories.headline: Senegal’s Pastef refuses participation in new government amid debt crisis turmoil

stories.body: The US Senate voted 52-47 along party lines to approve $70 billion in additional immigration enforcement money for DHS over three years without banning President Donald Trump’s $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund, despite failed bipartisan attempts to strip or reallocate it and Republican divisions over the politically risky provision ahead of November elections, Reuters reported.
stories.headline: Senate passes $70 billion ICE funding bill while leaving Trump’s “anti-weaponization” fund intact

stories.body: A unanimous panel of the 5th US Circuit Court of Appeals ruled that mifepristone must now be dispensed only in person at clinics, overruling FDA mail-order allowances on the grounds that shipments nullify Louisiana’s near-total abortion ban, a decision likely bound for the Supreme Court and the most significant federal restriction on medication abortion since Roe v. Wade was overturned, the Associated Press reported.
stories.headline: Appeals court blocks mailing of abortion pill prescriptions, tightening nationwide access

stories.body: Republican Governor Mike DeWine said data show capital punishment no longer deters violent crime and called for Ohio to repeal the death penalty, reversing the stance he championed 45 years ago while repeatedly postponing executions and citing the policy’s ineffectiveness at a news conference in Columbus, according to the Associated Press.
stories.headline: Ohio Governor Mike DeWine urges abolition of state death penalty

stories.body: FBI Director Kash Patel said agents disrupted planned attacks aimed at Sunday’s UFC exhibition on the White House South Lawn after learning of the threat on June 10, arresting multiple suspects from several states in a multi-agency operation amid reports—cited by Patel—that 23 conspirators allegedly intended to use explosive drones and mount a second-wave assault on the gates, according to Al Jazeera.
stories.headline: FBI says it foiled coordinated plot targeting UFC event at the White House

stories.body: Spain’s Congress Board, where left-wing parties hold a majority, refused to admit amendments from the PP and Junts that sought to compel lawmakers to demand Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez dissolve parliament, ruling that only the premier can call elections and frustrating the opposition’s attempt to frame the vote as a confidence question, El País reported.
stories.headline: Congress leadership blocks PP and Junts bid to force immediate general election vote

stories.body: National Court judge José Luis Calama rejected former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero’s bid to postpone questioning about a separate probe into jewels worth €1.3 million found in his Madrid office, finding no new facts beyond those in the broader Plus Ultra investigation into alleged influence peddling and thus no harm to his defense, El País reported.
stories.headline: Judge refuses Zapatero’s request to delay testimony over €1.3 million jewel trove

stories.body: During a preliminary hearing in the case against Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez’s wife, Begoña Gómez, presiding judge Juan Carlos Peinado heard requests from ultra-Catholic group Hazte Oír and other popular accusations to confiscate her passport, bar her from leaving Spain, and require biweekly court check-ins as they push for a jury trial over alleged influence peddling tied to businessman Juan Carlos Barrabés, El País reported.
stories.headline: Hazte Oír-led accusation seeks passport seizure for Begoña Gómez in corruption case

stories.body: The Madrid High Court’s sixth chamber suspended government soil tests at the Valley of Cuelgamuros—Franco’s former mausoleum—arguing the complex enjoyed top-level cultural heritage protection, even though the monument is not listed as a Bien de Interés Cultural under the regional catalog, a factual error confirmed by the regional culture department, El País reported.
stories.headline: Madrid court halts Cuelgamuros works after wrongly declaring the site protected

stories.body: As part of the RegenPorts program led by the BCN Port Innovation Foundation, the Port of Barcelona has begun deploying nearly 100 Ocean Ecostructures-designed underwater modules along docks and breakwaters to create colonization surfaces for marine organisms, with technical and scientific protocols intended to let operational ports worldwide replicate the biodiversity recovery effort, betevé reported.
stories.headline: Port of Barcelona installs underwater “Life Boosting Units” to restore marine biodiversity

stories.body: Barcelona’s Social Rights and Culture Commission approved an ERC proposal—supported by PSC, Junts, BComú and ERC—to contact newly registered residents with information on Catalan-learning resources, tighten enforcement of commercial signage rules, and offer sector-specific language training so that gentrifying neighborhoods maintain Catalan usage among businesses and neighbors, betevé reported.
stories.headline: Barcelona backs measures to reinforce Catalan use in gentrifying districts

stories.body: Deputy mayor Raquel Gil told the municipal social-services commission that the city currently houses 3,307 people from about 1,100 families—87% with children—in urgent accommodations such as pensions and hostels, a 12% rise over 2024 and 74% over 2021 driven by evictions, gender violence and migration pressures that now keep many cases in temporary lodgings for over six months, El Periódico reported.
stories.headline: Barcelona shelters more than 3,300 people in pensions to avert homelessness
